edges.description: Reduced cellular riboflavin uptake limits the precursor pool for synthesis of the flavin cofactors FMN and FAD.
Depletion of FAD/FMN deprives flavoprotein-dependent mitochondrial enzymes (electron transport chain complexes I and II, acyl-CoA dehydrogenases) of their essential cofactors.
Energy-demanding cranial-nerve motor nuclei, sensory neurons, and spinal motor neurons are especially vulnerable to flavoprotein-dependent mitochondrial failure, leading to their degeneration.

nodes.description: Pontobulbar (lower cranial nerve) palsy with dysphagia, dysarthria/anarthria, and facial and tongue weakness from degeneration of brainstem motor nuclei.

evidence.reference_title: The Brown-Vialetto-Van Laere and Fazio Londe syndrome revisited: natural history, genetics, treatment and future perspectives.
evidence.snippet: The most prevalent symptoms were bulbar palsy, hearing loss, facial weakness and respiratory compromise.

nodes.description: Progressive degeneration of lower cranial-nerve motor nuclei (pontobulbar palsy), sensory neurons (sensory ataxia, sensorineural/auditory neuropathy), spinal anterior-horn motor neurons, and optic and other long tracts. The resulting clinical picture is rapidly progressive axonal sensorimotor neuropathy with sensorineural hearing loss, bulbar palsy, optic atrophy, and respiratory insufficiency; an upper-motor-neuron component reminiscent of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis can also evolve.

evidence.reference_title: Treatable childhood neuronopathy caused by mutations in riboflavin transporter RFVT2.
evidence.snippet: Affected individuals share a core phenotype of rapidly progressive axonal sensorimotor neuropathy (manifesting with sensory ataxia, severe weakness of the upper limbs and axial muscles with distinctly preserved strength of the lower limbs), hearing loss, optic atrophy and respiratory insufficiency.

evidence.reference_title: Brown-Vialetto-Van Laere syndrome, a ponto-bulbar palsy with deafness, is caused by mutations in c20orf54.
evidence.snippet: The key features are progressive ponto-bulbar palsy and bilateral sensorineural deafness.

nodes.description: Riboflavin is the obligate precursor of the flavin cofactors flavin mononucleotide (FMN) and flavin adenine dinucleotide (FAD). Reduced riboflavin availability depletes these flavocofactors, which are essential prosthetic groups for the flavoprotein enzymes of mitochondrial energy metabolism.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, pathological and functional characterization of riboflavin-responsive neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: global knockdown of the single Drosophila melanogaster riboflavin transporter homologue revealed reduced levels of riboflavin, downstream metabolites, and electron transport chain complex I activity.

evidence.reference_title: Impaired riboflavin transport due to missense mutations in SLC52A2 causes Brown-Vialetto-Van Laere syndrome.
evidence.snippet: providing a rational basis for a high-dose riboflavin treatment.

nodes.description: High-dose oral riboflavin (vitamin B2) replenishes the deficient flavocofactor pool and is a life-saving, disease-modifying therapy. Clinical and biochemical improvement can be rapid (days to weeks) or gradual over months; early initiation, before genetic confirmation, is recommended because outcome depends on prompt treatment. Some deficits (e.g., established sensorineural hearing loss) may not reverse.

evidence.reference_title: Treatable childhood neuronopathy caused by mutations in riboflavin transporter RFVT2.
evidence.snippet: The clinical and biochemical responses of this SLC52A2-specific cohort suggest that riboflavin supplementation can ameliorate the progression of this neurodegenerative condition, particularly when initiated soon after the onset of symptoms.

evidence.reference_title: The Brown-Vialetto-Van Laere and Fazio Londe syndrome revisited: natural history, genetics, treatment and future perspectives.
evidence.snippet: All 13 patients who were treated with riboflavin survived, with a strong clinical improvement after days to months of treatment in eight patients.

evidence.reference_title: The Brown-Vialetto-Van Laere and Fazio Londe syndrome revisited: natural history, genetics, treatment and future perspectives.
evidence.snippet: treatment should be started immediately without first awaiting results of molecular analysis.

nodes.description: Loss of FAD/FMN cofactors impairs flavoprotein-dependent mitochondrial bioenergetics: reduced electron transport chain complex I and complex II activity, abnormal mitochondrial membrane potential and morphology, and impaired flavoprotein-dependent fatty-acid beta-oxidation (acyl-CoA dehydrogenation), the latter producing an abnormal acylcarnitine profile in some patients. The brainstem neuropathology resembles that of primary mitochondrial disease.

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, pathological and functional characterization of riboflavin-responsive neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: Electron transport chain complex I and complex II activity were decreased in SLC52A2 patient fibroblasts

evidence.reference_title: Clinical, pathological and functional characterization of riboflavin-responsive neuropathy.
evidence.snippet: showed classical symmetrical brainstem lesions resembling pathology seen in mitochondrial disease, including severe neuronal loss in the lower cranial nerve nuclei, anterior horns and corresponding nerves

nodes.description: Optic nerve atrophy with impaired visual acuity from optic neuropathy.

evidence.reference_title: Treatable childhood neuronopathy caused by mutations in riboflavin transporter RFVT2.
evidence.snippet: hearing loss, optic atrophy and respiratory insufficiency.

nodes.description: Progressive respiratory compromise from diaphragmatic and respiratory motor neuron involvement; diaphragmatic paralysis is a major cause of death.

evidence.reference_title: Treatable childhood neuronopathy caused by mutations in riboflavin transporter RFVT2.
evidence.snippet: characterized by progressive pontobulbar palsy, sensorineural hearing loss and respiratory insufficiency.

nodes.description: Biallelic loss-of-function mutations in the riboflavin transporter genes SLC52A3 (RFVT3; BVVL1) and SLC52A2 (RFVT2; BVVL2) reduce riboflavin transport activity at the plasma membrane, impairing cellular uptake of riboflavin from food (SLC52A3, with decreased plasma riboflavin) and from the blood into target cells (SLC52A2).

evidence.reference_title: Brown-Vialetto-Van Laere syndrome, a ponto-bulbar palsy with deafness, is caused by mutations in c20orf54.
evidence.snippet: We identified a candidate gene, C20orf54, by studying a consanguineous family with multiple affected individuals and subsequently demonstrated that mutations in this gene were the cause of disease in other, unrelated families.

evidence.reference_title: Impaired riboflavin transport due to missense mutations in SLC52A2 causes Brown-Vialetto-Van Laere syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Overexpression studies confirmed that the gene products of both mutant alleles have reduced riboflavin transport activities.

nodes.description: Progressive bilateral sensorineural deafness, due to auditory neuropathy spectrum disorder, is the most frequent presenting symptom and (when present) distinguishes BVVL from Fazio-Londe disease.

evidence.reference_title: Impaired riboflavin transport due to missense mutations in SLC52A2 causes Brown-Vialetto-Van Laere syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Progressive and severe sensorineural deafness is the most frequently observed presenting symptom.

evidence.reference_title: Auditory neuropathy in Brown-Vialetto-Van Laere syndrome due to riboflavin transporter RFVT2 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: Hearing loss in these individuals was due to auditory neuropathy spectrum disorder (ANSD).

nodes.description: Sensory ataxia from degeneration of sensory neurons and the posterior column-medial lemniscus / spinocerebellar pathways.

evidence.reference_title: Treatable childhood neuronopathy caused by mutations in riboflavin transporter RFVT2.
evidence.snippet: rapidly progressive axonal sensorimotor neuropathy (manifesting with sensory ataxia
